name: Borlenghe with Lardo
recipeIngredient: ¼ pound Lardo (available at Salumeria Biellese), plus 2 tablespoons
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, and egg. Add 2 cups water and beat with a whisk for 5 minutes or until very smooth.
2. Quickly stir in the remaining water. Allow to stand for 1 hour. (The batter will appear very thin, almost milk-like, but do not worry).
3. In a separate mixing bowl, stir together ¼ cup Lardo, Prosciutto, rosemary, garlic, and black pepper until smooth.
4. Heat a 14- to 16-inch non-stick pan over medium heat and brush with ¼ teaspoon remaining Lardo.
5. Pour enough batter into the pan to coat it (just like a crespella) and return to heat.
6. Cook for 4 minutes on the first side, then turn carefully. (The texture will not be like a crespella or anything else; this is an original and distinct delicacy. It may crack, but keep going).
7. Continue cooking until all the batter is finished, or about 10 to 12 borlenghe.
8. To serve, reheat the borlenghe quickly in the same pan, spread with the Prosciutto mixture, sprinkle with grated cheese, and serve warm, folded into quarters.
Yield: 4 servings
